LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND RICHARD WANNAVIROJ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION IN PART. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF THIS DATE AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT SHALL EITHER ISSUE A WRITTEN OORDER OR DIRECT COUNSELS TO DO SO. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR #5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PREVIOUSLY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON SEP-23-2016, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND RICHARD WANNAVIROJ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART, PER WRITTEN ORDER FILED OCT-11-2016. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Appearances:
Attorney, Daniel T. Pocklington, Esq. (925-295-0705) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Deputy City Attorney, Diana Rosenstein of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3933) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court and counsel view a video via CD-Rom of the incident leading up to this lawsuit. Defendant refers Court to frames ranging from 1840.47 through 1840.50 as indicative of plaintiff, Mr. Hopkins reaching a place of safety relative to a city bus. The Court vacates the tentative ruling.
